DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT RICHARD GROSSMAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. AS TO THE REQUEST FOR DOCUMENTS, THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO NOS. 6, 16, AND 18. AS TO THE INTERROGATORIES, THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO NOS. 6 AND 8. AS TO NOS. 12, 13, 14, 15, AND 21-23, THE MOTION IS DENIED AS THE REQUESTS ARE NOT RELEVANT AND THERE IS NO GOOD CAUSE TO INVADE PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO PRIVACY. FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE MADE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Responses To Interrogatories By Deft/X-Complainant, With Memo Of P&A Nad Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Jul-13-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: DANIEL T. BERNHARD, ESQ. OF FREELAND COOPER & FOREMAN, LLP, (415) 541-0200 FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, Richard Grossman.
ADAM CLAY KENT, ESQ., (650) 568-2800 FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY, JOHN GROSSMAN.
MATTER ARGUED. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Richard Grossman's Motion To Compel Production of Documents and Further Responses To Interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part.
As to the Request for Documents, the motion is granted as to Nos. 6, 16, and 18. Plaintiff shall respond and produce further, verifying that all responsive documents have been produced after conducting reasonable inquiry and diligent search.
As to the interrogatories, the motion is granted as to Nos. 6 and 8.
As to Nos. 12, 13, 14, 15, and 21-23, the motion is denied as the requests are not relevant and there is no good cause to invade Plaintiff's right to privacy.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. (612/HEWLETT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-03-2005 Set For Trial On Nov-07-05 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Oct-19-05 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 303
